rulings.Case Title: THOMAS NUSSBAUM VS. CAL INC.,A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce The Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 30, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANTS CAL INC., DAVID ESPARZA Motion To Enforce The Settlement Agreement. Motion continued to February 10, 2010. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS NUSSBAUM VS. CAL INC.,A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Re Notice) Of Motion For Court To Define Terms Of Settlement Agreement; And Other Defts To Submit The Proper Documents To Fbop As The Gsa Contract Holder
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 30, 2009, line 1, PLAINTIFF THOMAS NUSSBAUM Motion For Court To Define Terms Of Settlement Agreement. Motion continued to February 10, 2010. Parties must process another claim to assist Nussbaum in securing and obtaining the change orders from the Federal Bureau of Prisons and/or the GSA or any other appropriate branch of the federal government. Plaintiff is responsible for completing the standard Form 30 and supplying all information that defendant requires. Parties must meet and confer regarding the actions they will take in the event the claim is rejected or ignored. The claim submitted and any appeal must be submitted no later than February 5, 2010. By February 8, 2010 parties must file and serve joint status report regarding actions taken and progress made in resolving change order issue with a courtesy copy to this department. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR WEBB VS. UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Reconsideration (1) To Re-Open (2) For Equitable Relief After Judgment Denial Of Due Process Failure To Provide An Answer
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 30, 2009, line 3, PLAINTIFF ARTHUR WEBB Motion For Reconsideration (1) To Re-Open (2) For Equitable Relief After Judgment Denial Of Due Process Failure To Provide An Answer. Motion denied. Motion does not comply with code of civil procedure section 1008(b). Judgment of dismissal was signed by Judge Mahoney and filed February 13, 2009. The Court loses jurisdiction to rule on a motion for reconsideration when judgment is entered. See Apri Inc. Co. vs. Superior Court (1999) 76 Cal.App. 4th 176, 181. Prior reconsideration motion was denied and no new or different facts, circumstances, or law are raised by this reconsideration motion. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: TOM'S METAL SPECIALISTS, INC VS. WEST BAY BUILDERS, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-09-484299, CGC-09-489223, CGC-09-490551 WITH CASE CGC-08-477790
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 30, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANTS WEST BAY BUILDERS, INC, SAFECO INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-09-484299, CGC-09-489223, CGC-09-490551 WITH CASE CGC-08-477790. Motion to consolidate granted. Court orders consolidation for trial only. Parties to coordinate discovery and seek single assignment from Department 206. Lead case number for trial is 477790. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KOKO FUJITA FLOWERS VS. DAVID SEAN GILLIMORE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay Proceedings And Supporting Memorandum; Request For Judicial Notice Supporting Petition To Compel Arbitration; Appendix Of Non-California Authorities Supporting Petition To Compel Arbitration.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 30, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANTS VICTOR PERELLA, WELLS FARGO INVESTMENTS, LLC, WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. Notice Of Hearing on Petition To Compel Arbitration and Motion To Stay Proceedings. Petition is granted. Plaintiff has not established fraud in the execution or a fiduciary duty to orally disclose the existence of the arbitration agreement at the time of execution. Litigation stayed as to all defendants not subject to arbitration. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WINTERLAND SAN FRANCISCO PARTNERS A CALIFORNIA VS. GUIDO MARX et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 30, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANTS GUIDO MARX, CRAIG ANGELL DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Courts strictly read unlawful detainer notice requirements. CCP section 17(a) defines "person" to mean a natural person or a corporation. =(302/CWW)
